🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Barrington Court
*Country:* england
*Region:* south west
*County:* Somerset
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 16th century
*Coords:* 50.96111111, -2.85861111
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barrington_Court
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Barrington%20Court/@50.9417,-2.8417,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZNSGstGIqKGyqpcZOt0w_GrtEzry4wk_kmbNd83eMmYz8Tp6vEleqG5_528YgVqFlEEsWyDTS4Lo6Md7vD983ILU5RQqxPFnuOfMPs6jNjYNAH36x7FuHQFfccMn-PmTPcdhjGUDLLUc7WBctY=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* change google places link - https://maps.app.goo.gl/UpRwEgHiziz8AScn7


*Description:*
Barrington Court is a Tudor manor house begun around 1538 and completed in the late 1550s, with a vernacular stable court (1675), situated in Barrington, near Ilminster, Somerset, England.

_Listing 22 of 50_

listing.name: Barrington Court
listing.desc: Barrington Court is a Tudor manor house begun around 1538 and completed in the late 1550s, with a vernacular stable court (1675), situated in Barrington, near Ilminster, Somerset, England.

imageAttribution.description: South facade of Barrington Court, a Grade I listed Tudor style stately home in Somerset. Building commissioned in 1514 by Henry Daubeney, completed by 1558 for William Clifton, now owned by the National Trust.
